---
title: "3.7 final Constants and Static Song"
subtitle: "Class Variables and Methods"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit3-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## final Keyword

### Constant values

The keyword **final** can be used in front of a variable declaration to make it a constant value that cannot be modified.

Constants are traditionally capitalized.

```java
final double PI = 3.14;
```

## Coding Exercise

### `activecode:: finalPi`

Try the following code and notice the syntax error when we try to change the constant `PI`.

Put the comment symbols `//` in front of that line to remove the error and run it again.

## finalPi Starter

```java
public class TestFinal
{
    // A static final variable for PI
    public static final double PI = 3.14;

    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        System.out.println(PI);
        // TODO: Comment out the reassignment below to remove the syntax error
        PI = 4.2; // This will cause a syntax error
    }
}
```

## Test Target

### Runestone check

After the reassignment line is commented out, the expected output from `main` is:

```text
3.14
```

The classroom focus is that `final` prevents reassignment after initialization.

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### Static Song and counter

In Unit 1, we wrote a class with methods to print out the song The Ants Go Marching.

Here is the song with three verses, where the chorus is the last two lines of each verse.

The external song video and previous-lesson link from the source are omitted; the song text and coding task are retained.

## Song Text

### Three verses

::: {.scroll-block}
```text
Verse 1:
The ants go marching one by one, hurrah, hurrah
The ants go marching one by one, hurrah, hurrah
The ants go marching one by one
The little one stops to suck a thumb
And they all go marching down to the ground
To get out of the rain, BOOM! BOOM! BOOM! BOOM!

Verse 2:
The ants go marching two by two, hurrah, hurrah
The ants go marching two by two, hurrah, hurrah
The ants go marching two by two
The little one stops to tie a shoe
And they all go marching down to the ground
To get out of the rain, BOOM! BOOM! BOOM! BOOM!

Verse 3:
The ants go marching three by three, hurrah, hurrah
The ants go marching three by three, hurrah, hurrah
The ants go marching three by three
The little one stops to climb a tree
And they all go marching down to the ground
To get out of the rain, BOOM! BOOM! BOOM! BOOM!
```
:::

## Static Song Design

### Why static methods fit

Create a class to print out the song with two methods:

::: {.tight-list}
- `chorus()`
- `verse`
:::

The `verse` method takes two parameters for the numbers and the action.

This is a class where there are no instance variables and we do not really need to generate multiple objects.

With a class printing out a song, we can just make the methods static and have one copy of them.

## Challenge Requirements

### Step 1

Create a class called `Song` with two static methods:

::: {.tight-list}
- `chorus()`
- `verse()`
:::

The `chorus` method prints out the last two lines of each verse.

The `verse` method takes two parameters, a number and an action, and prints out the verse with the number and action.

## Challenge Requirements

### Step 2

Add a public static variable called **numVerses** to the class that keeps track of the number of verses.

Increment this variable in the method `verse` and print it out at the beginning of the verse.

## challenge-static-song Starter

### `activecode:: challenge-static-song`

```java
public class Song
{
    // Add a public static variable called numVerses

    // Add a static chorus method

    // Add a static verse method with two parameters

    public static void main(String args[])
    {
        // Print out the static variable numVerses
        // and call the static methods to print out the Song for 3 verses


    }
}
```

## Test Targets

### Static structure checks

The tests check for:

::: {.tight-list}
- a `verse` method header with two `String` parameters
- at least three calls to `Song.verse(`
- `public static int numVerses = 0`
- `numVerses++`
- a print statement that prints `numVerses`
:::

## Expected Output

### Test behavior

The output check compares the three verses from the source song text.

It ignores the printed verse numbers when matching the song lines, but it still checks that `numVerses` is incremented and printed.

## Expected Output Lines

### Song lines to preserve

Required song lines include:

::: {.tight-list}
- `The ants go marching one by one, hurrah, hurrah`
- `The little one stops to suck a thumb`
- `The ants go marching two by two, hurrah, hurrah`
- `The little one stops to tie a shoe`
- `The ants go marching three by three, hurrah, hurrah`
- `The little one stops to climb a tree`
- `To get out of the rain, BOOM! BOOM! BOOM! BOOM!`
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- explain why `final` prevents reassignment of `PI`
- fix `finalPi` by commenting out the reassignment line
- explain why a song-printing class can use static methods
- implement `chorus()` once and call it from `verse`
- implement `verse` with two `String` parameters
- use a shared static `numVerses` counter that increments and prints
:::

## End

### 3.7 complete

Next: scope and access.
